marketState: neutral, conflicting signals — bearish trend/storage vs bullish weather catalyst and extreme-short positioning
summary: Front (Aug) is trading essentially at fair value ($2.842 vs $2.923 fair, band $2.42-$3.42), so there is no large mispricing to lean on today. Seasonality is mildly bearish (price -9.7pp below its typical seasonal path, weak 38% odds of being higher in 3-4 weeks) but reliability is explicitly LOW this week, so it carries little weight. The most striking input is weather: near-term (1-7D) demand is only mildly above normal (+3.2%), but the 8-16D outlook is running +25.4% vs 5-year norms and sits at the 96th percentile of its range — a real bullish demand catalyst building just past the short-term horizon. That's offset by a storage surplus of +180 Bcf vs 5-year with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf), and technicals that are uniformly bearish (trend down, 50<200 EMA) on every timeframe from 5-minute to weekly. Managed money is near a 5-year extreme net-short (8th percentile), which is a classic contrarian setup for a squeeze if the weather catalyst materializes, but it hasn't shown up in price action yet.

longTerm.rationale: The 8-16D weather demand spike (+25.4% vs 5y, 96th percentile) and an extreme managed-money net-short (8th percentile 5y) both argue for upside risk, and fair value drifts up to $3.01 by +21D suggesting the market expects firmer prices into fall. But every technical timeframe (5m-1w) is still trending bearish with 50<200 EMAs, seasonality reliability is explicitly low this week, and the storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y, bearish surprise) caps conviction on a rally right now.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily/weekly close below $2.55 with storage surprises staying bearish would confirm the downtrend and rule out the long case.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for daily/weekly trend confirmation (RSI reclaiming >45, MACD histogram turning positive on 4h/1d) or a pullback into the $2.55-$2.65 zone, ideally alongside the 8-16D heat signal holding or strengthening, before entering long.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; take partial profit at TP1
shortTerm.rationale: Near-term (1-7D) weather demand is only mildly above normal (+3.2%, mid-range) so there's no near-term bullish catalyst yet, while technicals are bearish across every intraday-to-4h timeframe with bearish EMA structure, and the front is essentially fair-valued with only a small (-$0.08) discount. Sep trades at a small backwardation to Aug (-$0.017), consistent with a soft near-term tape.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and hold above $3.00, or an early pull-forward of the 8-16D heat signal into the 1-7D window, would invalidate the short.

Managed money net-short at 8th percentile of 5y range — crowded short, squeeze risk if weather/price turns

risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly LOW this week (59pp 10-90 spread), so the seasonal read could easily be wrong
